title: Offline Voice Context Stack
subtitle: Physical audio becomes agent-ready only after durable transport, speech evidence, provenance, and policy control.

nodes.label: Physical Capture
nodes.description: User-visible recording state, local audio, timestamps, and device identity.
nodes.evidence: Capture must remain recoverable when the network is absent.

nodes.label: Resilient Sync
nodes.description: Sequenced chunks, checksums, retries, and resumable transfer over available links.
nodes.evidence: Store-and-forward transport separates capture reliability from connection quality.

nodes.label: Recording State
nodes.description: Create, receive, finalize, version, and emit durable lifecycle events.
nodes.evidence: Uploaded and finalized are distinct states.

nodes.label: Speech Evidence
nodes.description: ASR, timestamps, confidence, language, and speaker diarization.
nodes.evidence: Generated conclusions should retain links to transcript and timing evidence.

nodes.label: Context Object
nodes.description: Typed artifacts, provenance, participants, permissions, and retention state.
nodes.evidence: Versioned provenance makes correction and reprocessing possible.

nodes.label: Governed Action
nodes.description: Draft, confirm, commit, audit, and roll back according to authority.
nodes.evidence: Agent confidence does not replace an action policy.

edges.label: verified bytes
edges.evidence: Checksums and idempotent retries protect integrity.

edges.label: finalized audio
edges.evidence: Speech processing begins from a canonical recording boundary.

edges.label: attributed evidence
edges.evidence: Transcript spans and speaker turns feed a typed context object.

edges.label: permissioned intent
edges.evidence: Allowed uses and authority constrain downstream automation.
